pairs.question: Who is Butch Soper and what are some of his notable achievements in professional bowling?
pairs.answer: Butch Soper is a retired professional 10-pin bowler who competed on the PBA Tour for 29 years. He won 6 titles, including one major and a mixed doubles victory, with 11 runner-up finishes and 15 top-5 appearances. His career highlights include a perfect 300 game in 1994 and his sole major win at the 1996 PBA National Championship.

pairs.question: How many years did it take Butch Soper to win his first PBA Tour tournament after joining the tour?
pairs.answer: It took Butch Soper 6 years to win his first PBA Tour tournament. He captured his maiden victory at the 1978 Fair Lanes Open after joining the tour in 1972.

pairs.question: What was significant about Soper's perfect 300 game at the 1994 Hilton Hotels Classic?
pairs.answer: Soper's perfect 300 game at the 1994 Hilton Hotels Classic was notable as it was the 8th perfect game aired on live television. Additionally, he became the first bowler to bowl a televised 300 to defeat another player who had previously bowled a 300 on TV, as his opponent Bob Benoit had done so at the 1988 PBA Quaker State Open.

pairs.question: Describe the matches leading to Butch Soper's final PBA Tour victory at the 1996 PBA National Championship.
pairs.answer: Soper qualified for the final rounds of the 1996 PBA National Championship as the #2 seed. He won his semifinal match against Justin Hromek with a score of 216–214 and then triumphed over Walter Ray Williams, Jr. in the title match with a score of 226–210.

pairs.question: What was Butch Soper's lone major victory on the PBA Tour?
pairs.answer: Butch Soper's lone major victory on the PBA Tour was at the 1996 PBA National Championship.

pairs.question: Did Butch Soper win any titles on the PBA Senior Tour? If so, which one?
pairs.answer: Yes, Butch Soper won one title on the PBA Senior Tour. He secured the victory at the 2000 PBA Seattle Senior Open, defeating Norb Wentzel 222-210 in the championship match.

pairs.question: What hall of fame is Butch Soper a member of?
pairs.answer: Butch Soper is a member of the Orange County USBC Hall of Fame.
